entries.title: Dashboard
entries.purpose: Use Dashboard to monitor sales, stock, approvals, and alerts so you can decide the next operational action quickly.

prerequisites.check: User session is authenticated in the correct business context.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Dashboard and check branch/date filters, alerts, and KPI cards to identify the next operational priority.
workflow.expected_result: Dashboard is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Review KPI cards, low-stock highlights, and recent activity widgets for the active branch.
workflow.expected_result: The main dashboard operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Apply date or branch filters, then drill into the metric that needs follow-up.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that the selected dashboard metric drill-down opens the intended target module, then continue to Search to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Dashboard handoff to Search is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Dashboard, action failed with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED; requested update was not applied.
common_errors.likely_cause: The Dashboard request failed backend validation for BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED.
fix_steps: Correct the invalid input/state related to BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED on Dashboard.
Retry the same Dashboard action in the same branch/business context.
If it persists, escalate with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED and request payload from /{locale}.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Dashboard, action failed with USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS; requested update was not applied.
common_errors.likely_cause: The Dashboard request failed backend validation for USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS.
fix_steps: Correct the invalid input/state related to USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS on Dashboard.
Retry the same Dashboard action in the same branch/business context.
If it persists, escalate with USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS and request payload from /{locale}.

related_pages.reason: Login must be completed before Dashboard so required context and records exist.
Search is the next operational page after Dashboard to continue or verify outcomes.
Reports is the next operational page after Dashboard to continue or verify outcomes.
Stock is the next operational page after Dashboard to continue or verify outcomes.
Notifications is the next operational page after Dashboard to continue or verify outcomes.
Offline is the next operational page after Dashboard to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Approvals
entries.purpose: Use Approvals to review and decide pending requests that require supervisory confirmation before they affect operations.

prerequisites.check: Role can view approvals queue.
To approve or reject requests, role also needs required permission.
Active branch/business selection matches the records you are reviewing.

workflow.step: Open Approvals and filter by status, action type, search text, and date range to isolate the pending items that need action.
workflow.expected_result: Approvals list is narrowed to the relevant pending requests.

workflow.step: Open each pending request, review requester, target, reason, and quantity/amount details, then approve or reject.
workflow.expected_result: Decision is submitted and the request status updates from PENDING.

workflow.step: If a request is no longer pending, refresh the list and continue with the next visible pending record.
workflow.expected_result: Queue reflects current server state with no stale items.

workflow.step: After decisions are complete, continue to the downstream operational page (for example Purchase Orders or Transfers) to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Handoff is clear and next operation can continue with updated approval state.

common_errors.error_symptom: Approve/reject action is blocked or fails with a forbidden/permission error.
common_errors.likely_cause: Current role is missing approvals.write.
fix_steps: Ask an admin to grant approvals.write in Settings - Roles.
Re-login or refresh the page after permissions are updated.
Retry the approve/reject action from /{locale}/approvals.

common_errors.error_symptom: Approving a stock-related request fails with stock service unavailable.
common_errors.likely_cause: Approval execution depends on stock service and it is temporarily unavailable.
fix_steps: Wait briefly and retry the approval action.
Verify stock module health and connectivity.
If it persists, escalate with approval ID and timestamp.

common_errors.error_symptom: Approving a stock adjustment/count request fails with an invalid payload message.
common_errors.likely_cause: Stored approval payload is incomplete or malformed for stock execution.
fix_steps: Reject the broken request with a note so requester can submit again.
Create a new request from the source stock page with complete fields.
Escalate with approval ID and payload context if the issue repeats.

related_pages.reason: Settings - Users must be completed before Approvals so required context and records exist.
Purchase Orders is the next operational page after Approvals to continue or verify outcomes.
Transfers is the next operational page after Approvals to continue or verify outcomes.

workflow.step: Preview, replace, or remove outdated files while preserving required compliance evidence.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that attachment links and file states match the target record requirements, then continue to Notes to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Attachments handoff to Notes is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Attachments, action failed with ATTACHMENT_EXCEEDS_20MB_LIMIT; requested update was not applied.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Attachments, action failed with ATTACHMENT_TYPE_MUST_BE_JPG_PNG_OR_PDF; requested update was not applied.

fix_steps: Correct the invalid input/state related to ATTACHMENT_TYPE_MUST_BE_JPG_PNG_OR_PDF on Attachments.
Retry the same Attachments action in the same branch/business context.
If it persists, escalate with ATTACHMENT_TYPE_MUST_BE_JPG_PNG_OR_PDF and request payload from /{locale}/attachments.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Attachments, action failed with STORAGE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED; requested update was not applied.
common_errors.likely_cause: The Attachments request failed backend validation for STORAGE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED.
fix_steps: Correct the invalid input/state related to STORAGE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED on Attachments.
Retry the same Attachments action in the same branch/business context.
If it persists, escalate with STORAGE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and request payload from /{locale}/attachments.

related_pages.reason: Catalog - Products must be completed before Attachments so required context and records exist.
Notes is the next operational page after Attachments to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Audit Logs
entries.purpose: Use Audit Logs to trace user actions and system events for investigations, compliance, and incident review.

prerequisites.check: Role can view audit logs and export audit results.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Audit Logs and apply actor/date/module filters to isolate the exact event trail you need.
workflow.expected_result: Audit Logs is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Filter logs by actor, module, date range, and event type to isolate the exact activity window.
workflow.expected_result: The main audit logs operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Inspect event payload details and correlate related actions across connected records.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that the event trail clearly identifies actor, action, and affected record, then continue to Exports to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Audit Logs handoff to Exports is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Audit Logs, records are blocked for the selected branch because of branch scope restriction.
common_errors.likely_cause: The selected branch is outside the user's allowed branch scope.
fix_steps: Select a branch allowed by your role scope.
If the branch should be visible, request branch-scope update from administrator.
Escalate with role details, branchId, and filter payload if restriction continues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Audit Logs, the action is denied and audit list/export does not complete.
common_errors.likely_cause: Role is missing required permission (audit.read) for this action.
fix_steps: Request audit.read permission for your role.
Sign out and sign in again to refresh permission claims.
Retry audit list or export after permissions are updated.

related_pages.reason: Dashboard must be completed before Audit Logs so required context and records exist.
Exports is the next operational page after Audit Logs to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Catalog - Categories
entries.purpose: Use Categories to define product grouping rules that drive product organization, search, and reporting.

prerequisites.check: You need permission to view catalog pages.
To create, edit, or disable categories, you need catalog edit access.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Catalog Categories and validate naming/status rules before creating or editing category records.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Categories is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Create or edit category records with clear names and active status for operational use.
workflow.expected_result: The main catalog - categories operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Review category usage across products before disabling or deleting a category.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that category records are active and ready for product assignment, then continue to Catalog - Products to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Categories handoff to Catalog - Products is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Categories, save fails with NAME_IS_REQUIRED and the category is not created or updated.
common_errors.likely_cause: Category name was empty when submitting create/update.
fix_steps: Enter a category name, then submit again.
Keep the same business context and retry.
If it still fails, escalate with NAME_IS_REQUIRED and the request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Categories, request fails with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED before changes are applied.
common_errors.likely_cause: Business context was missing while calling category endpoints.
fix_steps: Re-select the correct business/branch context.
Refresh the page and retry the same category action.
If it persists, escalate with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED and session details.

related_pages.reason: Catalog - Products is the next operational page after Catalog - Categories to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Catalog - Products
entries.purpose: Use Products to create and maintain sellable items with correct category, pricing, and stock behavior settings.

prerequisites.check: You need permission to view catalog pages.
To create or edit products and product images, you need catalog edit access.
At least one active category exists for product assignment.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Catalog Products and verify branch/catalog context before creating or editing items.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Products is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Create or update product details, assign category, and configure core selling attributes.
workflow.expected_result: The main catalog - products operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Attach images, confirm identifiers, and validate product state for downstream POS and stock flows.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that products are saved with correct category, units, and media links, then continue to Catalog - Variants to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Products handoff to Catalog - Variants is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products, save fails with CATEGORYID_IS_REQUIRED and the product is not created.
common_errors.likely_cause: No category was selected when submitting product creation.
fix_steps: Select a category and submit again.
If no category exists, create one first in Catalog - Categories.
If it still fails, escalate with CATEGORYID_IS_REQUIRED and request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products, image upload fails with PRIMARY_IMAGE_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: The first image was submitted as non-primary.
fix_steps: Set the first uploaded image as primary.
Upload again and confirm primary status is checked.
If it persists, escalate with PRIMARY_IMAGE_IS_REQUIRED.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products, adding another image fails with subscription-limit error.
common_errors.likely_cause: Current subscription tier does not allow extra product images.
fix_steps: Keep only the primary image on the product.
Remove extra image attempts and save again.
If needed, upgrade subscription for additional images.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products, image upload fails with IMAGE_EXCEEDS_20MB_LIMIT.
common_errors.likely_cause: Image file size is above 20 MB upload limit.
fix_steps: Compress or resize the image below 20 MB.
Upload the reduced file and retry.
If it still fails, escalate with IMAGE_EXCEEDS_20MB_LIMIT and file metadata.

related_pages.reason: Catalog - Categories must be completed before Catalog - Products so required context and records exist.
Catalog - Variants is the next operational page after Catalog - Products to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Catalog - Products - Wizard
entries.purpose: Use Product Wizard for guided multi-step product setup when creating complete product records with fewer mistakes.

prerequisites.check: User session is authenticated in the correct business context.
To create products, variants, and barcodes in this wizard, you need catalog edit access.
If you post initial stock from this wizard, you need stock adjustment access.
At least one active category exists for product assignment.
Units are configured for variant base/sell unit selection.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Product Wizard and complete all setup steps in sequence from basics through review.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Products - Wizard is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Move through each wizard step in order, filling required fields before advancing.
workflow.expected_result: The main catalog - products - wizard operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Review summary step for category, pricing, and variant readiness before final submission.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that wizard output produced a complete and valid product record, then continue to Catalog - Variants to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Products - Wizard handoff to Catalog - Variants is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products - Wizard, completion fails with CATEGORYID_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: Product step was submitted without a category.
fix_steps: Return to product step and select a category.
Continue through review and submit again.
If it persists, escalate with CATEGORYID_IS_REQUIRED and wizard payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products - Wizard, variant creation fails because unit fields are missing.
common_errors.likely_cause: Variant step left base unit or sell unit empty.
fix_steps: Set both base unit and sell unit for each variant.
Recheck all drafted variants before submit.
Retry wizard completion with corrected unit values.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Products - Wizard, variant save fails with CONVERSION_FACTOR_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: Base and sell units differ but conversion factor is missing or invalid.
fix_steps: Enter a positive conversion factor when units differ.
Ensure factor is numeric and greater than zero.
Retry wizard completion after correction.

related_pages.reason: Catalog - Categories must be completed before Catalog - Products - Wizard so required context and records exist.
Catalog - Variants is the next operational page after Catalog - Products - Wizard to continue or verify outcomes.
Stock - Adjustments is the next operational page after Catalog - Products - Wizard to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Catalog - Variants
entries.purpose: Use Variants to define product options such as size or color that affect stock units and pricing behavior.

prerequisites.check: You need permission to view catalog pages.
To create/edit variants and run barcode/SKU changes, you need catalog edit access.
Parent products already exist and are active.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Catalog Variants and select the parent product before managing variant records.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Variants is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Create variant combinations and set unique identifying fields for each sellable unit.
workflow.expected_result: The main catalog - variants operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Validate barcode/lookup behavior and availability for POS and stock transactions.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that variant records are selectable in stock and sales workflows, then continue to Stock to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Catalog - Variants handoff to Stock is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Variants, update or action fails with VARIANT_NOT_FOUND.
common_errors.likely_cause: Target variant no longer exists in the active business context.
fix_steps: Refresh and reselect the variant from current list.
Confirm you are in the correct business/branch context.
If still missing, escalate with VARIANT_NOT_FOUND and variant ID.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Variants, barcode lookup fails because the same code matches multiple variants.
common_errors.likely_cause: Barcode uniqueness is broken across active variant records.
fix_steps: Review barcode assignments and make each active barcode unique.
Use barcode reassignment flow where needed.
Retry lookup after resolving duplicates.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Variants, barcode reassignment submit fails because reason is missing.
common_errors.likely_cause: Reassignment request was sent without a reason text.
fix_steps: Enter a clear reason in the reassignment dialog.
Submit again with the same target variant.
If it persists, escalate with REASON_IS_REQUIRED_FOR_BARCODE_REASSIGNMENT.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Catalog - Variants, SKU reassignment submit fails because reason is missing.
common_errors.likely_cause: SKU reassignment request was sent without reason text.
fix_steps: Enter reason in SKU reassignment prompt.
Resubmit the SKU reassignment.
If it persists, escalate with REASON_IS_REQUIRED_FOR_SKU_REASSIGNMENT.

related_pages.reason: Catalog - Products must be completed before Catalog - Variants so required context and records exist.
Stock is the next operational page after Catalog - Variants to continue or verify outcomes.

Required role permissions for this action are configured in Role Settings.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.expected_result: Customers is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Merge duplicates or archive inactive profiles while preserving transaction history links.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that customer profile changes are visible and usable at checkout, then continue to Pos to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Customers handoff to Pos is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Customers, create fails with NAME_IS_REQUIRED and the customer is not saved.
common_errors.likely_cause: Customer name was empty during create request.
fix_steps: Enter a customer name and submit again.
Keep the same business context and retry.
If it persists, escalate with NAME_IS_REQUIRED and request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Customers, list or save request fails with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: Session business context was missing while calling customers endpoints.
fix_steps: Re-select the correct business context.
Reload Customers and retry the same action.
If it persists, escalate with BUSINESS_CONTEXT_REQUIRED.

related_pages.reason: Dashboard must be completed before Customers so required context and records exist.
Pos is the next operational page after Customers to continue or verify outcomes.
Receipts is the next operational page after Customers to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Expenses
entries.purpose: Use Expenses to record operating costs and keep business spending visible for reporting and controls.

prerequisites.check: To view expense lists and filters, role needs expenses read access.
To create new expense records, role also needs expenses write access.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.

workflow.step: Open Expenses and capture cost entries with correct amount, category, branch, and posting date.
workflow.expected_result: Expenses is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Capture expense entries with amount, category, date, and branch context.
workflow.expected_result: The main expenses operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Attach proof documents and verify totals appear correctly in finance-related reporting.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that expense posting is reflected in financial summaries and reports, then continue to Reports to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Expenses handoff to Reports is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Expenses, save failed with INVALID_EXPENSE_CATEGORY.
common_errors.likely_cause: Selected category value is not one of the allowed backend expense categories.
fix_steps: Select a valid category from the predefined expense category options.
Submit again after category is corrected.
If still failing, escalate with category value and request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Expenses, create action is blocked while request is marked as offline.
common_errors.likely_cause: Expense creation is intentionally forbidden in offline mode by controller policy.
fix_steps: Reconnect to online mode before submitting expense create.
Retry expense submission after network and offline flags are cleared.
If it still appears online, escalate with request headers and client offline state.

related_pages.reason: Settings - Business must be completed before Expenses so required context and records exist.
Reports is the next operational page after Expenses to continue or verify outcomes.
Dashboard is the next operational page after Expenses to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Exports
entries.purpose: Use Exports to generate downloadable business data files for accounting, audit, and offline analysis.

prerequisites.check: Role can create/list/download export jobs.
Active branch/business selection matches the target records for this task.
For branch-scoped users, choose a valid in-scope branch when creating branch-specific exports.

workflow.step: Open Exports, choose dataset and filters, then submit export jobs for the required period.
workflow.expected_result: Exports is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Choose export type, date range, and filters, then submit the export job.
workflow.expected_result: The main exports operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Monitor job status and download output when processing completes.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that export job status reaches completion with downloadable output, then continue to Audit Logs to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Exports handoff to Audit Logs is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Exports, job creation failed with UNSUPPORTED_EXPORT_TYPE.
common_errors.likely_cause: The selected export type is not allowed by backend export handlers.
fix_steps: Choose one of the supported export types shown in the Exports form.
Retry job creation after updating the type.
If it persists, escalate with the attempted type and request body.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Exports, audit export failed because acknowledgement was not provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: Audit log exports require explicit acknowledgement before processing.
fix_steps: Enable the acknowledgement confirmation for audit export.
Submit the export job again after acknowledgement is set.
If still blocked, escalate with export type AUDIT_LOGS and request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Exports, action failed due to branch-scope restriction.
common_errors.likely_cause: The requested branch is outside the user's allowed scope, or no valid branch was supplied for branch-scoped export.
fix_steps: Pick a branch that is in your allowed scope.
For branch-scoped export requests, provide a valid branchId.
Escalate with role, branch scope, and request payload if restriction persists.

related_pages.reason: Reports must be completed before Exports so required context and records exist.
Audit Logs is the next operational page after Exports to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Invite
entries.purpose: Use Invite to send onboarding invitations to users who need access to the business workspace.

prerequisites.check: Valid invite token is present in the invite link (token query parameter).
Invite token has not expired and has not been accepted before.
Name and password are provided, and password meets policy requirements.

workflow.step: Open Invite and send role-scoped invitations to users who need workspace access.
workflow.expected_result: Invite is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Enter recipient details, select role scope, and send the invitation.
workflow.expected_result: The main invite operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Track invite status and resend or revoke invitations that are pending too long.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that invite state is sent, pending, or resolved as expected, then continue to Login to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Invite handoff to Login is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Invite, acceptance fails because invite token is expired or no longer valid.
common_errors.likely_cause: Invite token is expired, already used, or no longer matches an open invitation.
fix_steps: Request a fresh invitation link from an administrator.
Open the newest invite link and submit again.
If problem persists, escalate with invitation email and timestamp.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Invite, account setup fails because the password does not satisfy policy.
common_errors.likely_cause: Password does not meet required length/complexity rules.
fix_steps: Create a stronger password that matches displayed requirements.
Submit invite acceptance again with the updated password.
If still blocked, escalate with password policy version and request context.

related_pages.reason: Settings - Users must be completed before Invite so required context and records exist.
Login is the next operational page after Invite to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Login
entries.purpose: Use Login to authenticate a user account and start a secure business session in the correct business context.

prerequisites.check: User has a registered account email and the correct password.
If the account belongs to multiple businesses, user can choose the correct business during login.

workflow.step: Open Login and enter email plus password for the intended account.
workflow.expected_result: Credentials are submitted and authentication begins.

workflow.step: If prompted, select the target business from the business picker and continue.
workflow.expected_result: Business context is selected for this session.

workflow.step: Wait for successful sign-in and session initialization with device-bound tokens.
workflow.expected_result: Access token, refresh token, and user session are created.

workflow.step: If onboarding is incomplete, continue to Onboarding; otherwise proceed to Dashboard.
workflow.expected_result: User lands on the correct next route for their setup state.

common_errors.error_symptom: Login request fails and sign-in is rejected with INVALID_CREDENTIALS.
common_errors.likely_cause: Email or password does not match a valid account.
fix_steps: Re-enter email and password carefully and retry login.
If password is forgotten, start password reset flow before retrying.
If issue persists, confirm account email exists in the target business.

common_errors.error_symptom: Login request is rejected with EMAIL_IS_NOT_VERIFIED.
common_errors.likely_cause: Account is still pending email verification.
fix_steps: Open Verify Email flow from the verification link or token.
Request a new verification email if token expired or missing.
After verification succeeds, return to Login and retry.

common_errors.error_symptom: Login returns USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS after business resolution.
common_errors.likely_cause: User is not an active member of the selected business.
fix_steps: Select the correct business if multiple options are available.
Ask an admin to reactivate your business membership in Settings - Users.
Retry login after membership status is ACTIVE.

related_pages.reason: Verify Email may be required before Login for pending accounts.
Onboarding is the next route when business setup is incomplete after Login.
Dashboard is the next route when onboarding is already complete.

entries.title: Notes
entries.purpose: Use Notes to capture operational reminders and context that teams can reference during daily work.

prerequisites.check: To open notes lists and metadata, role needs notes read access.
To create, edit, archive, or schedule reminders, role also needs notes write access.
To manage notes across users/branch scope, use notes manage permission.

common_errors.likely_cause: Receiving payload has zero complete lines.

If still failing, escalate with RECEIVING_MUST_INCLUDE_AT_LEAST_ONE_LINE and payload.

common_errors.likely_cause: Backend forbids receiving posts in offline mode.

If online but still blocked, escalate with RECEIVING_IS_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_OFFLINE_MODE.

common_errors.likely_cause: Neither purchaseId nor purchaseOrderId was included in request.
fix_steps: Select a purchase or purchase order source before submit.

Escalate with PURCHASEID_OR_PURCHASEORDERID_IS_REQUIRED if source is selected but not sent.

common_errors.likely_cause: PO status is not APPROVED or PARTIALLY_RECEIVED.
fix_steps: Approve the PO first in Purchase Orders.
Retry receiving only after PO status is valid.
If status is valid but still blocked, escalate with PURCHASE_ORDER_NOT_APPROVED_FOR_RECEIVING and PO ID.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Receiving, submit fails when line quantities/costs differ from PO and no reason is provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: An override condition was detected without override reason.
fix_steps: Provide override reason when receiving differs from PO expectation.
Correct line values if override is not intended.
Escalate with RECEIVING_OVERRIDE_REQUIRES_A_REASON if reason is supplied but rejected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Receiving, submit fails because a line variant is not part of the referenced PO.

fix_steps: Use only variants that exist on the selected PO.

Escalate with VARIANT_NOT_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER and variant/PO IDs if unexpected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Receiving, submit fails because a batch code is missing when batch tracking applies.
common_errors.likely_cause: Batch tracking is enabled and line was sent without batch code.
fix_steps: Enter batch code for each tracked line.
Include expiry date if your process requires it.
Escalate with BATCH_CODE_IS_REQUIRED_FOR_RECEIVING if batch code was entered but still rejected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Receiving, submit fails because referenced batch ID does not match branch/variant context.
common_errors.likely_cause: Provided batchId is invalid for this business, branch, or variant.

Escalate with BATCH_NOT_FOUND_FOR_RECEIVING_LINE and batch/variant/branch IDs if needed.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Receiving list/filter, load fails for branches outside your scope.

Escalate with BRANCH_SCOPED_ROLE_RESTRICTION and branch ID when unexpected.

fix_steps: Select a valid variant before submitting the adjustment.
Confirm branch + variant context is correct.
If it still fails, escalate with VARIANTID_IS_REQUIRED and request payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Adjustments, negative adjustment fails because no loss reason was supplied.
common_errors.likely_cause: Negative adjustment type requires a loss reason in backend validation.
fix_steps: Choose a loss reason when posting a negative adjustment.
Retry using the same branch and variant context.
If still blocked, escalate with LOSS_REASON_IS_REQUIRED_FOR_NEGATIVE_ADJUSTMENTS.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Adjustments, submit fails because the selected batch cannot be found.
common_errors.likely_cause: batchId does not exist for the active business/branch context.
fix_steps: Reload available batches and reselect a valid batch.
Confirm the batch belongs to current branch and variant.
Escalate with BATCH_NOT_FOUND and batch/branch/variant IDs if needed.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Adjustments, negative adjustment fails because resulting stock would go below zero.
common_errors.likely_cause: Business stock policy does not allow negative stock.
fix_steps: Reduce adjustment quantity or replenish stock first.
Re-check current stock snapshot before retry.
Escalate with NEGATIVE_STOCK_IS_NOT_ALLOWED and stock snapshot if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Adjustments, list load fails for branches outside your scope.
common_errors.likely_cause: Selected branch is not permitted by branch-scoped role restrictions.
fix_steps: Switch to a permitted branch.
Request branch scope update if required.
Escalate with BRANCH_SCOPED_ROLE_RESTRICTION and branch ID if unexpected.

related_pages.reason: Stock must be completed before Stock - Adjustments so required context and records exist.
Stock - Movements is the next operational page after Stock - Adjustments to continue or verify outcomes.

entries.title: Stock - Counts
entries.purpose: Use Stock Counts to record cycle counts and compare counted quantities against system balances.

prerequisites.check: To open count sessions and history, your role allows stock viewing.
To submit stock counts, your role allows stock editing.
Counting scope is set and counted quantities are captured before final submit.

workflow.step: Open Stock Counts and start/select a count session before entering physical quantities.
workflow.expected_result: Stock - Counts is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Create count session and capture physical quantities for selected items.
workflow.expected_result: The main stock - counts operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Review variances and prepare approvals/adjustments based on findings.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that count session variances are saved for reconciliation, then continue to Stock - Counts - Wizard to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Stock - Counts handoff to Stock - Counts - Wizard is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts, submit fails because counted quantity was not provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: countedQuantity is empty, null, or not numeric.
fix_steps: Enter counted quantity as a valid number.
Verify unit selection and submit again.
If still failing, escalate with COUNTEDQUANTITY_IS_REQUIRED and payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts, submit fails because counted quantity is negative.
common_errors.likely_cause: Backend rejects count sessions with counted quantity below zero.
fix_steps: Change counted quantity to zero or a positive number.
Review physical count entry and re-submit.
Escalate with COUNTED_QUANTITY_CANNOT_BE_NEGATIVE if unexpected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts, submit is blocked when offline mode is enabled.
common_errors.likely_cause: Stock counts endpoint forbids offline posting.
fix_steps: Return online and retry count submission.
Keep draft notes locally and post once connected.
If still blocked online, escalate with STOCK_COUNTS_ARE_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_OFFLINE_MODE.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts, submit fails because referenced batch is not found.
common_errors.likely_cause: Selected batch is invalid for current business/branch/variant context.
fix_steps: Reload batch list and reselect a valid batch.
Confirm branch and variant filters before submit.
Escalate with BATCH_NOT_FOUND and batch/variant/branch IDs if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts, list/filter load fails for branch outside role scope.
common_errors.likely_cause: Branch-scoped role restriction blocks selected branch.
fix_steps: Switch to a branch you are allowed to access.
Request admin update if you need wider branch scope.
Escalate with BRANCH_SCOPED_ROLE_RESTRICTION and branch ID if unexpected.

related_pages.reason: Stock must be completed before Stock - Counts so required context and records exist.
Stock - Counts - Wizard is the next operational page after Stock - Counts to continue or verify outcomes.
Stock - Movements is the next operational page after Stock - Counts to continue or verify outcomes.

prerequisites.check: To load wizard stock/batch context, your role allows stock viewing.
To submit wizard counts, your role allows stock editing.

workflow.step: Open Stock Count Wizard and complete scope, count, and review steps in order.

workflow.expected_result: The main stock - counts - wizard operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Confirm final count submission once all mandatory steps are complete.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that wizard count submission is finalized and traceable, then continue to Stock - Movements to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Stock - Counts - Wizard handoff to Stock - Movements is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts - Wizard, submit fails because one or more counted quantities are missing.
common_errors.likely_cause: Wizard line items include empty or non-numeric countedQuantity values.
fix_steps: Complete counted quantity on all required lines.
Re-run review step and submit again.
Escalate with COUNTEDQUANTITY_IS_REQUIRED and line payload if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts - Wizard, submit fails because a counted quantity is negative.
common_errors.likely_cause: At least one wizard line has value below zero.
fix_steps: Set all counted quantities to zero or above.
Check line-by-line values before submit.
Escalate with COUNTED_QUANTITY_CANNOT_BE_NEGATIVE if unexpected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts - Wizard, submit is blocked in offline mode.
common_errors.likely_cause: Stock count posting is online-only for this flow.
fix_steps: Reconnect and retry submission.
Keep temporary notes until online state is restored.
Escalate with STOCK_COUNTS_ARE_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_OFFLINE_MODE if still blocked online.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Counts - Wizard, submit fails because a selected batch cannot be found.
common_errors.likely_cause: Batch reference is invalid for selected branch/variant scope.
fix_steps: Refresh batch options and reselect valid batch records.
Verify branch and variant choices before submit.
Escalate with BATCH_NOT_FOUND and context IDs if unresolved.

related_pages.reason: Stock - Counts must be completed before Stock - Counts - Wizard so required context and records exist.
Stock - Movements is the next operational page after Stock - Counts - Wizard to continue or verify outcomes.

workflow.step: Open Stock Movements and filter by item/date/type to trace quantity change history.

workflow.step: Trace source transactions linked to each movement entry.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Stock - Movements, load/filter fails for a branch outside your role scope.

entries.title: Supplier Returns
entries.purpose: Use Supplier Returns to send damaged or incorrect stock back to suppliers with proper documentation.

prerequisites.check: To open and review supplier returns, your role allows procurement viewing.
To create supplier returns, your role allows procurement editing.
Branch and supplier context is selected for the return document.

workflow.step: Open Supplier Returns and add eligible return lines with reason and quantity against supplier records.
workflow.expected_result: Supplier Returns is loaded with the intended operational scope and ready for the task.

workflow.step: Create return records with supplier, items, quantities, and return reasons.
workflow.expected_result: The main supplier returns operation is accepted and returns valid status feedback.

workflow.step: Confirm return status transitions and stock deductions tied to return posting.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that return transactions are posted with correct stock impact, then continue to Reports to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Supplier Returns handoff to Reports is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns, submit fails because no return lines were provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: The return payload has no valid lines.
fix_steps: Add at least one complete return line (variant, quantity, and cost).
Remove blank rows before submit.
Escalate with RETURN_MUST_INCLUDE_AT_LEAST_ONE_ITEM and payload if still failing.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns, submit fails for manual returns that have no reason.
common_errors.likely_cause: At least one line has no receiving reference and return reason is empty.
fix_steps: Provide a clear reason for manual return submissions.
If return is based on receiving lines, attach receiving references correctly.
Escalate with MANUAL_SUPPLIER_RETURNS_REQUIRE_A_REASON if reason is present but still rejected.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns, submit fails because a referenced receiving line does not exist.
common_errors.likely_cause: receivingLineId is invalid or no longer available.
fix_steps: Reload receiving data and reselect the source receiving line.
Check that branch/source filters still match the selected line.
Escalate with RECEIVING_LINE_NOT_FOUND and receivingLineId if issue persists.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns, submit fails because receiving line source does not match selected purchase or PO.
common_errors.likely_cause: The referenced receiving line belongs to a different purchase or purchase order.
fix_steps: Use receiving lines that belong to the same selected purchase/purchase order.
Clear source filters and reselect matching records.
Escalate with RECEIVING_LINE_DOES_NOT_MATCH_RETURN_SOURCE and involved IDs if needed.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns, submit fails because return quantity exceeds available stock.
common_errors.likely_cause: Negative stock is not allowed and current stock is insufficient.
fix_steps: Reduce return quantity to available stock amount.
Check stock movements before retrying return.
Escalate with INSUFFICIENT_STOCK_FOR_SUPPLIER_RETURN and stock snapshot if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Supplier Returns list/filter, load fails for branches outside your scope.
common_errors.likely_cause: Requested branch is not in your role branch scope.
fix_steps: Switch to a permitted branch.
Request branch-scope update from admin if needed.
Escalate with BRANCH_SCOPED_ROLE_RESTRICTION and branch ID if this is unexpected.

related_pages.reason: Stock - Movements must be completed before Supplier Returns so required context and records exist.

workflow.step: Open Suppliers and maintain supplier profile, status, and terms used in procurement flows.

workflow.step: Archive inactive suppliers while keeping historical purchase links intact.
workflow.expected_result: Dependent records update consistently with no integrity or sequencing conflicts.

workflow.step: Verify that supplier status and terms are ready for procurement use, then continue to Purchase Orders to proceed with the next workflow step.
workflow.expected_result: Suppliers handoff to Purchase Orders is ready with no blocking validation issues.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Suppliers, save fails because supplier name was not provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: The supplier create request was submitted with an empty or blank name.
fix_steps: Enter a non-empty supplier name and save again.
Keep contact fields optional, but do not leave the name blank.
If the form still fails, escalate with NAME_IS_REQUIRED and the submitted payload.

entries.purpose: Use Transfers to move stock between branches with clear source, destination, and quantity control.

prerequisites.check: To open transfer lists and pending queues, your role allows transfer viewing.
To create/approve/receive/cancel transfers, your role allows transfer editing.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, create/approve/receive/cancel actions are blocked in offline mode.
common_errors.likely_cause: Transfers endpoints enforce online-only operations.
fix_steps: Reconnect and retry transfer action.
Confirm device is online and sync is healthy.
Escalate with TRANSFERS_ARE_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_OFFLINE_MODE if still blocked online.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, create fails because no transfer items were provided.
common_errors.likely_cause: Create payload has empty items array.
fix_steps: Add at least one transfer line before submitting.
Ensure lines remain after filtering/editing.
Escalate with ITEMS_ARE_REQUIRED and payload if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, create fails because one or more item quantities are invalid.
common_errors.likely_cause: At least one quantity is not numeric.
fix_steps: Set all item quantities to valid numbers.
Remove empty/invalid line entries and retry.
Escalate with ITEM_QUANTITY_MUST_BE_A_NUMBER and offending payload.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, create fails because source branch stock is insufficient.
common_errors.likely_cause: Requested transfer quantity exceeds available source stock.
fix_steps: Reduce quantity or replenish source branch stock.
Verify snapshot quantities before retry.
Escalate with INSUFFICIENT_STOCK_FOR_TRANSFER and variant/branch context.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, create fails because selected batch does not match source branch/variant.
common_errors.likely_cause: Batch belongs to different branch/variant than transfer line.
fix_steps: Select a batch from the same source branch and variant.
Clear batch and re-pick after confirming branch/variant.
Escalate with BATCH_DOES_NOT_MATCH_SOURCE_BRANCH_VARIANT and IDs if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, receive action fails because transfer is not approved yet.
common_errors.likely_cause: Receive was attempted while transfer status is still REQUESTED.
fix_steps: Approve transfer first.
Retry receive after status moves to APPROVED/IN_TRANSIT.
Escalate with TRANSFER_MUST_BE_APPROVED_BEFORE_RECEIVING and transfer ID if needed.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, receive/cancel fails because transfer is already closed.
common_errors.likely_cause: Transfer status is COMPLETED or CANCELLED.
fix_steps: Open a new transfer for additional movement.
Do not retry receive/cancel on closed transfer.
Escalate with TRANSFER_IS_ALREADY_CLOSED if status display seems wrong.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, receive fails because requested quantity exceeds remaining quantity.
common_errors.likely_cause: Receive quantity is greater than unreceived balance for one or more items.
fix_steps: Set receive quantity to remaining quantity or less.
Reopen line details and verify already received amounts.
Escalate with RECEIVED_QUANTITY_EXCEEDS_REMAINING_QUANTITY and item IDs if unresolved.

common_errors.error_symptom: On Transfers, list or action fails for branches outside your role scope.

Request admin update to branch scope when operationally required.
Escalate with BRANCH_SCOPED_ROLE_RESTRICTION and branch IDs if unexpected.

entries.title: Verify Email
entries.purpose: Use Verify Email to confirm account ownership and activate pending account membership before full login access.

prerequisites.check: Valid verification token is available from verification email link or copied token.
If available, keep the businessId/email context from the verification link to simplify routing.

workflow.step: Open Verify Email from the email link or paste token manually.
workflow.expected_result: Token and optional business context are loaded for verification.

workflow.step: Submit token to email-verification confirm endpoint.
workflow.expected_result: Token is validated and user email is marked verified.

workflow.step: If device context is available, session may be created immediately; otherwise user is redirected to Login.
workflow.expected_result: User is either auto-signed-in or routed to Login with context hints.

workflow.step: Continue with Login or directly to app shell depending returned session state.
workflow.expected_result: Verified account can proceed with authenticated workflow.

common_errors.error_symptom: Verification fails with VERIFICATION_TOKEN_EXPIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: Token is invalid, already used, or expired.
fix_steps: Request a fresh verification email from Verify Email page.
Use the newest token/link and retry verification.
If multiple emails exist, verify you opened the latest message.

common_errors.error_symptom: Verification succeeds but session creation fails with USER_NOT_ACTIVE_FOR_THIS_BUSINESS.
common_errors.likely_cause: Resolved business membership is not ACTIVE for this user.
fix_steps: Try logging in and select a different valid business membership if offered.
Ask admin to activate membership for the target business.
Retry verification/login after membership activation.

related_pages.reason: Signup commonly precedes Verify Email for new owner accounts.
Login is the next route when verification completes without auto-session.
Dashboard is the next route when verification returns immediate session tokens.
